After six days travel they reached high ground over the junction of the Wollondilly and Wingecarribee rivers, from where they saw the open country beyond the mountains. A month later, Wilson and Price, this time accompanied by Henry Hacking and a man called Collins, set out again and this time reached the summit of Mount Towrang, where they looked over the Great Divide.

John Price’s diaries of the two expeditions were handed over to Hunter, who gave them to Sir Joseph Banks in England. The diaries languished among Banks's papers for many years until they were acquired by the government of New South Wales, and published as an appendix to Volume III of the Historical Records of New South Wales in 1895. R. H. Cambage then meticulously traced the routes of the two journeys in a paper read to the Royal Australian Historical Society in 1919. Cambage mistakenly identified the author of the diaries as one 'Barracks' (hence his confusion when the name Price crops up in the diary), but John Price’s authorship and part in the expeditions was finally established in 1960 by A. H. Chisholm in The Romance of the Lyrebird, who credited Wilson with the discovery of the lyrebird.

My reading of Price's diaries and Cambage's commentary on them was inspired by Chris Cunningham’s The Blue Mountains Rediscovered, which sets out to scotch the myth that the ‘heroes’ Blaxland, Lawson and Wentworth were the first to cross the Blue mountains and look on the interior in 1813. They had probably been crossed several times before and I was pleased to discover that the leader of the first documented crossing was a Lancashire man, or at least a man who had made his way to Australia from the Wigan assizes, having been sentenced to transportation for the theft of nine yards of velveret to the value of tenpence. (Summary by Phil Benson)

title: Prime Minister
description: The Prime Minister is the fifth in Trollope's series of six Palliser novels. With Phineas' difficulties resolved, Trollope introduces new characters. A respectable young girl forsakes the man her family had always intended her to marry when she falls in love with a man of foreign extraction and an unknown family. He has a gentleman's education and manners, but his family background and financial means are mysterious. Is he really a gentleman? Meanwhile, Plantagenet Palliser becomes Prime Minister of a shaky coalition government, and Glencora and Madame Goessler are busy with the ensuing social obligations. (Summary by Karen Merline) The other Palliser can be found at - 1 - Can You Forgive Her? 2 - Phineas Finn, the Irish Member 3 - The Eustace Diamonds 4 - Phineus Redux 5 - The Prime Minister 6 - The Duke’s Children

title: Phineas Redux
description: Phineas Finn is the fourth in Trollope's series of six Palliser novels. At the end of Phineas Finn, the second novel in the series, Phineas had returned to Ireland and married his childhood sweetheart after having left the House of Commons. As Phineas Redux opens, Phineas is working as a Poorhouse Inspector in Ireland. His wife having died in childbirth, he finds his existence dull and unsatisfying. Phineas' returns to England; his career advances and his romantic adventures continue, while we encounter many familiar characters including Glencora and Plantagenet Palliser, Madame Goesler, and Lizzie Eustace and her husband the Reverand Mr. AEmelius. (Summary by Karen Merline) Warning! Do not read the Wikipedia article linked to the title of this novel unless you are prepared for a SPOILER!!! LibriVox recordings of other novels in the series are available: 1-Can You Forgive Her? 2-Phineas Finn, the Irish Member 3-The Eustace Diamonds 4-Phineas Redux 5-The Prime Minister 6-The Duke’s Children

title: The Real World Tarot Podcast
author: Izolda Trakhtenberg
description:
A woman sat down across from me at a Renaissance Festival and asked about her writer's block. The cards didn't say a word about her book. They said: go to the doctor. Tomorrow. Whatever is going on in your lower abdomen, deal with it now. She came back a year later with a bouquet of flowers and the book she wrote about surviving stage four ovarian cancer.

That's what tarot actually does when you use it right.

The Real World Tarot Podcast is your weekly guide to the most practical, purposeful, and honest tarot system ever created. The Functional Tarot deck has 84 cards, six more than any traditional deck, including Process, Purpose, Passion, Envy, Adventure, and Meditation cards, plus an entirely new suit: the Suit of Voices, built specifically to help you find and live your full purpose through your work and your lifetime.

Every episode, we go deep on what the cards are actually saying, how to read them with honesty and precision, and how this ancient system can give you the insight, clarity, and direction you've been looking for, not someday, but right now.

If you want fortune-telling, this isn't your show. If you want to know yourself better, act with more purpose, and finally stop waiting to live the life you're meant to live, pull up a chair. The cards are ready.

title: Do You Hear This?
author: Aaron
description: Christian based topics with devotionals, interviews, testimonies, concert news.

title: He Knew He Was Right
description: He Knew He Was Right is a 1869 novel written by Anthony Trollope which describes the failure of a marriage caused by the unreasonable jealousy of a husband exacerbated by the stubbornness of a willful wife. As is common with Trollope's works, there are also several substantial subplots. Trollope considered this work to be a failure; he viewed the main character as unsympathetic, and the secondary characters and plots much more lively and interesting. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Don Sebastian; or, The House of the Braganza: An Historical Romance, Volume 2
description: Don Sebastian is now king of Portugal, and, although exiled, looks forward to taking his seat on the throne. However, he is held captive by the beautiful Kara Aziek. Tempting as it is to stay and admire her, he knows the path to true happiness lies in freedom and endeavors to escape. The journey through Africa and across the sea is hazardous, but he must attempt it if he is to rule his people. - Summary by Lynne T.

title: Rupert of Hentzau
description: This is the sequel to 'The Prisoner of Zenda'. Five years have passed. The King has become jealous of Rudolf Rassendyll and suspicious of the queen (Flavia)'s feelings towards him. Flavia decides that this must be the last year in which she sends to Rudolf the single red rose that betokens her love, and therefore she also sends via Fritz von Tarlenheim, her letter of good-bye.Count Rupert of Hentzau, banished from Ruritania after the incidents of the earlier book, is plotting his return. In furtherance of his scheme he obtains both letter and rose, and plots to place them before the King. Rudolf, Fritz and Sapt must prevent this at all costs... (Summary by Andy)

title: Comedies of Courtship
description: A series of short stories about love and the process of courtship. - Summary by Michele Eaton

title: Chick-fil-A Play
author: Chick-fil-A Play
description: Welcome to Chick‑fil‑A Play™—a place where stories, sounds, and imagination bring families together. Chick‑fil‑A Play offers family‑friendly audio series created for co‑listening, featuring original stories and adventures designed to spark curiosity, kindness, and connection. Whether you’re listening in the car, at home, or on‑the‑go, each episode invites kids and families to enjoy meaningful moments together through playful storytelling and heart‑warming themes.

title: When Woman Proposes
description: Does love at first sight exist? The heroine of this story, Natalie, seems to think so and is willing to move heaven and earth in order to marry Captain Mowbray whom she notices at a reception dinner. Thankfully Natalie is rich, brave, and willing to stand up for what she believes in. She will need every ounce of courage and every dollar she has in order to see herself become the bride of her handsome Captain who refuses to ever become a married man. - Summary by Jenn Broda

title: Mrs. Dymond
description: Susanna Holcombe, a very sensitive and free spirited young lady, tries to fit in to society. But it is very hard for a Victorian woman to carve her own path, and she is almost forced to marry colonel Dymond. This book tells about her trials and tribulations. Can she find her place at last? Perfect for fans of Mary Elizabeth Braddon and Anthony Trollope, and those who want a Jane Austen novel with a twist. Anne Isabella Thackeray Ritchie was the eldest daughter of William Makepeace Thackeray, author of Vanity Fair. - Summary by Stav Nisser.

title: Gulzara; or The Persian Slave
description: “Gulzara, The Persian Slave” is a rare example of a script for a Victorian melodrama that was intended as a private theatrical – to be performed by a limited cast of amateurs in a home or school, not on a public stage. Rarer still, “Gulzara” was written by a female author to be performed by a cast of women. The only male character, Amurath, was a “breeches role,” played by a young girl. Thus this drama, set in a harem in Constantinople, in addition to spinning a yarn about the abduction of Sultan Suliman’s son, gives us an unusual peek at the lives of young women in the U.S. in the 1840’s and 50’s. - Summary by Kelly S. Taylor Cast List Amurath Jasmin Salma Zuleika Kelly S. Taylor Fatima the story girl Katinka Larry Wilson Gulzara E. J. Lavery Ayesha TJ Burns Stage Directions Wayne Cooke

title: House in the Mist
description: It was a night to drive any man indoors. Not only was the darkness impenetrable, but the raw mist enveloping hill and valley made the open road anything but desirable to a belated wayfarer like myself. Being young, untrammeled, and naturally indifferent to danger, I was not averse to adventure; and having my fortune to make, was always on the lookout for El Dorado, which, to ardent souls, lies ever beyond the next turning. Consequently, when I saw a light shimmering through the mist at my right, I resolved to make for it and the shelter it so opportunely offered. But I did not realize then, as I do now, that shelter does not necessarily imply refuge, or I might not have undertaken this adventure with so light a heart. Yet, who knows? The impulses of an unfettered spirit lean toward daring, and youth, as I have said, seeks the strange, the unknown and, sometimes, the terrible. (Exerpt from Chapter I)

title: Amethyst Box
description: On the evening before his marriage, Sinclair loses a precious curiosity from his collection: an amethyst box, containing a tiny flask of deadly poison. He suspects that this poison is in the possession of either his betrothed or her cousin, the girl his best friend Worthington loves. Turning to Worthington for help, they try to recover the box before the poison can be administered... (Summary by Carolin)

title: Mayor's Wife
description: Miss Saunders is out for an adventure. One, which is full of secrets, hints, and half-lies. One, which will require all of her wits. She is to be the companion to the Mayor's wife. The Lady is unhappy, and the reason for her grave unhappiness is more serious than you think. (Summary by Stav Nisser)

title: Burglar and the Blizzard
description: Geoffrey Holland, a young millionaire, visits his country home in Hillsborough after hearing of a recent string of burglaries. To Holland's surprise, he finds the burglar reading a book in his library. And it's none other than his old schoolmate, Billy McVay. As the blizzard gets stronger, McVay persuades Holland to go out and rescue his sister who is living in a nearby rundown shack. What will Holland do and find? - Summary by SaraHale

title: Selling What You Make
author: Andy
description: A practical podcast for makers & producers

title: EDWY: A Poem, in Three Parts
description: In Edwy, Ann Radcliffe gives us a delightful piece of poetic moonshine, whose eponymous hero seeks assistance from the world of faerie in order to spy on his girlfriend, Aura, and see if she really loves him. He does this by venturing unseen into Windsor Forest at night to trap the love-fay, Eda, who, once spellbound, must reveal all and let him remotely view Aura's activities by means of a magic mirror cut from crystal. In addition to this early form of cyberstalking, Edwy, on his night-journey into the forest gets to witness a royal procession of the Fairie Queen, followed by midnight revels of elves and spirits. What seems like a mid-summer night's dream turns into a comedy of errors, though, as Edwy suffers memory loss, makes a false start, and unintentionally looses a mischievous wood sprite in the course of his romantic quest. Puckishly playful, Mrs. Radcliffe imbues her tale with a remarkable sense of the glimmering feyness of the nighttime forest, making a principal appeal of the poem's dreamlike world the inscrutability and ultimate indeterminacy of events glimpsed by moonlight. (Summary by Grant Hurlock)

title: Castles of Athlin and Dunbayne
description: Ann Radcliffe is the founder of the gothic novel. This novel is no exception. The wicked baron murdered the good earl's father twelve years before the novel began. Only twelve years later, free from his mother's wishes, can the earl seek revenge. Meanwhile, Mary, the earl's beautiful sister is falling in love with a peasant. Yet her brother was abducted by the baron and he wants to marry her. She may have to wed him in order to secure his return. We see Mary's conflict along with a description of her brother's captivity. This book has everything: murder, revenge, battles, damsels in distress, aristocrats, captives, secrets, and, most of all, love. It is a love story in every sense: the love to the Scottish highlands in which it is set, the family love that binds almost above all else, and of course the love between men and women. This short work would later influence Radcliffe's other works. Radcliffe had an influence on Jane Austen, Mary Shelley, and many others who wrote popular gothic novels. - Summary by Stav Nisser and Wikipedia

title: Texas Matchmaker
description: As a boy Andy Adams helped with the cattle and horses on the family farm. During the early 1880s he went to Texas, where he stayed for 10 years, spending much of that time driving cattle on the western trails. A Texas Matchmaker is a narrative that describes the work of a cowboy on a large southTexas ranch during the late 1800’s. Adams is considered to be one of foremost writers of the life of the real American cowboy. (Summary by Richard Kilmer)

title: Antiquary
description: Illegitimacy, false identity, and bankruptcy are the major elements of Sir Walter Scott's 1816 novel, The Antiquary. Set in the period of the French Revolution, the novel's hero, Lovel, struggles to gain repute and the hand of his beloved despite his uncertain parentage. During these pursuits, he befriends the title's antiquary, Johnathan Oldbuck, who finds Lovel a captive audience to his scholarly studies and a tragic likeness to his own disappointments in love. Readers will discover whether Lovel's acts of bravery and courage ultimately earn him the birth and fortunes of a nobleman. (Summary by S. Kovalchik)

title: Carved Cupboard (Dramatic Reading)
description: Agatha, Gwen, Clare and Elfie have always been told that they will inherit their aunt's house. But when their aunt dies, she leaves it all to their intolerable cousin James. What will they do? Will the verses Nannie gives them prove true? Summary by Esther ben Simonides. Cast List:Narrator: Larry WilsonAgatha: Beth ThomasGwen: Adele de PignerollesClare: Esther ben SimonidesElfie, Maid: RachelCousin James: Zames CurranHugh Knox: tovarischNannie: KHandJane: Vanessa CooleyPatty: MaryAnnDeb: April WaltersMiss Miller: Kristin GjerløwThe Pastor, Walter, Watson, Roger Lester, Clement Arkwright, the Footman: Rupert HollidayMiss Villars: Allie ChristyAudrey Foster, Curate's Daughter: LydiaTom Lester: OxenhandlerMajor Lester: BillAlick Lester: Bob ScottAudio Edited by: Esther ben Simonides

title: Adeline Mowbray
description: Everybody makes mistakes, and everything has a price. This novel describes, according to its name, the life of Adeline Mowbray, full of everything: sorrow, happiness, falsehood, truth, kindness, and mistakes. This novel is an exploration of the human heart. Be prepared for a strong and enjoyable read. (Summary by Stav Nisser)

title: Maid of Maiden Lane (dramatic reading)
description: The Maid of Maiden lane is a wonderful love story in which Mrs. Barr intertwines the hot political and social issues that were occurring in America during the last decade of the 18th century with an excellent love story plot. Some of those issues include: the moral dilemma and debate over the French Revolution, and how that event touched the lives of the immigrants in America; the prejudices between the immigrants from England, and those from France or Holland, and how those animosities affected the ordinary lives of the people; and the political debate over titles, foreign policy, and such things(for example)as where the capital of the nation was to reside, New York or Philadelphia. The author gives us a picture of New York City that is vastly different from today, with it's residential areas and tree lined roads as the backdrop for this very interesting drama. Cornelia, the Maiden of Maiden lane, is loved by two young men. Who she chooses, and the obstacles that the two face because of the opposition of their fathers, friends, and a huge mistake that turns everything upside down, propels the story towards its climax, where a surprise character, spiritual in nature, comes to the rescue, while sacrificing her own desire for love to help the two lovers. Good character, love, and the family ties and relationships that existed during those times are very well portrayed by the author, and the book is as interesting from a historical point of view as it is from a truly interesting and sweet old fashioned love story. (Summary by Robert Hoffman) Cast:Narrators: Tiffany Halla Colonna and Beth Thomas Cornelia Moran: Grace George Hyde: Chuck Williamson Arenta Van Ariens: Amanda Friday Ava Moran: CaprishaPage Annie Hyde: Victoria Martin Aunt Angelica: Elizabeth Klett Richard Hyde: Ron Altman Kate Hyde: MJ Franck Joris Van Heemskirk: Algy Pug Lysbet Van Heemskirk: Kristin Gjerløw John Moran: Robert Hoffman Rem Van Ariens: Michael Reuss Peter Van Ariens: David Lawrence Jacobus Van Ariens: ToddHW an Englishman: Beth Thomas John Adams: ToddHW Mrs. Adams: April Gonzales Mrs. Smith: KateC Thomas Jefferson: Ken Garrett Mrs. Wiley: KateC Mary Damer: Charlotte Duckett Anthony Clymer: Nathanial W.C. Higgins Doctor Roslyn: Nathanial W.C. Higgins Captain Jacobus: Nathanial W.C. Higgins Prooflisteners: Tisha Bordon, Beth Thomas and David Lawrence Audio edited by: Robert Hoffman, ToddHW and David Lawrence

title: Irene Iddesleigh
description: Amanda McKittrick Ros, a Northern Irish writer, did for the novel what William McGonagall did for poetry and Florence Foster Jenkins for the coloratura voice. She published a number of novels (all at her own expense) and in addition to being a novelist was a poet, her best known being Visiting Westminster Abbey which begins: Holy Moses! Take a look! Flesh decayed in every nook! Some rare bits of brain lie here, Mortal loads of beef and beer. The best account of her life available on the web is probably http://oddbooks.co.uk/amanda/l.... C. S. Lewis, J. R. R. Tolkien and The Inklings were admirers and held competitions to see who could read her work for the longest time whist keeping a straight face. Sadly, all her books are now out of print but command high prices on the rare books market. Irene Iddesleigh, her first book, is a romantic novel, telling the common story of an unhappy, doomed marriage. The prose style however is unique. (Summary by AJM)

title: Garthowen: A Story of a Welsh Homestead
description: The story centers around the Owens' family and young ‘Morva of the Moor’, the prettiest girl in the world. The Owens of Garthowen, once a prestigious local family, are on a financial decline and they are now considered locally, to be simple farmers. Morva Lloyd works for the Owens' family as a shepherdess, a milk maid and general helper. But is also considered to be a family member and was fostered by Sara, the neighbour. She is also loved by both of Ebben Owens sons, Will and Gethin, in slightly different ways! This is the engaging tale of the family's complex and rolling situation. - Summary by Craig Franklin

title: Goodness of St. Rocque and Other Stories
description: These stories focus on the Creole society of New Orleans and in the process reveals issues facing black Americans at the end of the nineteenth-century. - Summary by Jim Locke

title: Priceless Pearl
description: Pearl Leavitt is habitually fired from her New York City office jobs for being "too beautiful" and thereby causing all the men to fall in love with her. Fed up, she decides to take a job in the Hamptons as a governess for three over-indulged children. - Summary by Nancy Halper

title: Come Out of the Kitchen!
description: A novel about a man who rents a house for the season and keeps having problems with the servants, especially the attractive cook. - Summary by Margaret

title: Innocencia: a story of the prairie regions of Brazil
description: The story of Innocencia, an 18-year-old girl who lives in the prairies of Brazil, is a twist on the traditional love triangle. The plot has been compared to the more famous "Paul and Virginie" and "Romeo and Juliet", but it takes place on the dropback of the loneliness of the sparsely populated backregions in 19th century Brazil, visited by a German naturalist in search of new species of insects, Dr. Meyer, who unsuspectedly finds himself caught in a complicated maze of jealousy, love and distrust. Inocencia was the first book by a Brazilian writer to be translated into English, as the translator states in his preface. - Summary by Leni

title: Princess
description: The Princess is a serio-comic blank verse narrative poem, written by Alfred Tennyson, published in 1847. The poem tells the story of an heroic princess who forswears the world of men and founds a women's university where men are forbidden to enter. The prince to whom she was betrothed in infancy enters the university with two friends, disguised as women students. They are discovered and flee, but eventually they fight a battle for the princess's hand. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Idylls of the King
description: Idylls of the King, published between 1856 and 1885, is a cycle of twelve narrative poems by the English poet Alfred, Lord Tennyson which retells the legend of King Arthur, his knights, his love for Guinevere and her tragic betrayal of him, and the rise and fall of Arthur's kingdom. The whole work recounts Arthur's attempt and failure to lift up mankind and create a perfect kingdom, from his coming to power to his death at the hands of the traitor Mordred. Individual poems detail the deeds of various knights, including Lancelot, Geraint, Galahad, and Balin and Balan, and also Merlin and the Lady of the Lake. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Camille
description: The Lady of the Camellias (French: La Dame aux camélias) is a novel by Alexandre Dumas, fils, first published in 1848, that was subsequently adapted for the stage. The Lady of the Camellias premiered at the Theatre de Vaudeville in Paris, France on February 2, 1852. An instant success, Giuseppe Verdi immediately set about to put the story to music. His work became the 1853 opera La Traviata with the female protagonist "Marguerite Gautier" renamed "Violetta Valéry". In the English-speaking world, The Lady of the Camellias became known as Camille and sixteen versions have been performed at Broadway theatres alone. The "lady of the camellias" is Marguerite Gautier, who is based on Marie Duplessis, the real life lover of author Dumas, fils. (Summary from Wikipedia)

title: Ten Years Later
description: After The Three Muskateers and Twenty Years After the adventurous story of Athos, Porthos, Aramis and D'Artagnan continues!The Vicomte of Bragelonne: Ten Years Later (French: Le Vicomte de Bragelonne ou Dix ans plus tard) is the last of the Musketeer novels. It is usually divided into four volumes and this second volume contains chapters 76-140. (Summary by Diana Majlinger)

title: Louise de la Valliere
description: After The Three Muskateers and Twenty Years After the adventurous story of Athos, Porthos, Aramis and D'Artagnan continues! The Vicomte of Bragelonne: Ten Years Later (French: Le Vicomte de Bragelonne ou Dix ans plus tard) is the last of the Musketeer novels. It is usually divided into four volumes and this third volume contains chapters 141-208. (Summary by Diana Majlinger)

title: Three Musketeers
description: The Three Musketeers (Les Trois Mousquetaires) is a novel by Alexandre Dumas, père. It recounts the adventures of a young man named d'Artagnan after he leaves home to become a musketeer. D'Artagnan is not one of the musketeers of the title; those are his friends Athos, Porthos, and Aramis -- inseparable friends who live by the motto, "One for all, and all for one". The Three Musketeers was first published in serial form in the magazine Le Siècle between March and July 1844. Dumas claimed it was based on manuscripts he had discovered in the Bibliothèque Nationale. It was later proven that Dumas had based his work on the book Mémoires de Monsieur D'Artagnan, capitaine lieutenant de la première compagnie des Mousquetaires du Roi (Memoirs of Mister D'Artagnan, Lieutenant Captain of the first company of the King's Musketeers) by Gatien de Courtilz de Sandras (Cologne, 1700). Dumas' version of the story covers the adventures of D'Artagnan and his friends from 1625 to 1628, as they are involved in intrigues involving the weak King Louis XIII of France, his powerful and cunning advisor Cardinal Richelieu, the beautiful Queen Anne of Austria, her English lover, George Villiers, 1st Duke of Buckingham, and the Siege of La Rochelle. Adding to the intrigue are the mysterious Milady de Winter, and Richelieu's right-hand man, the Comte de Rochefort. (summary from wikipedia) Get Twenty Years After here. Get The Man in the Iron Mask here.

title: d'Artagnan Romances, Vol 1: The Three Musketeers (version 3)
description: The d'Artagnan Romances are six volumes that intertwine exciting events (real and fictionalized) from 17th-century Europe and the life of the most adored French musketeer of that period, Charles de Batz-Castelmore d'Artagnan. Volume 1 introduces d’Artagnan as a young man, seeking to earn the glory of serving in the King’s Musketeers. While in pursuit of this aim, d’Artagnan befriends three musketeers – Athos, Porthos, and Aramis – who aid him in adventures against their King’s adversary, the cunning Cardinal Richelieu. "One for all, and all for one!" - Summary by jvanstan

title: Vicomte De Bragelonne
description: After The Three Muskateers and Twenty Years After the adventurous story of Athos, Porthos, Aramis and D'Artagnan continues!The Vicomte of Bragelonne: Ten Years Later (French: Le Vicomte de Bragelonne ou Dix ans plus tard) is the last of the Musketeer novels. It is usually divided into four volumes and this first volume contains chapters 1-75. (Summary by Diana Majlinger)

title: Hoag MD Podcast
author: Hoag Health
description: Hoag MD – a podcast where we’ll dive deep into the backgrounds of the talented physicians being recruited to Orange County, and the groundbreaking clinical work being done at the highest ranked hospital in Orange County.

title: The Dr. Reginald W. Steele Podcast
author: reginaldsteele78
description:
New podcast weblog

When No One Leads the Church is a podcast for pastors, ministry leaders, and faithful members who long to see their congregations transformed. Rooted in theological reflection and lived pastoral experience, Dr. Reginald W. Steele explores the intersection of ecclesial challenges, power dynamics, and sustained survival modalities, and how spiritually grounded, shared leadership can bring renewal. If you care deeply about the future of the church, this conversation is for you.

title: Curio Cabinet
author: Selin Khalil
description: Step into the shadows where secrets whisper and time bends. This is a journey through untold stories, where every silence hides a revelation.

title: Raised Hope Podcast
author: Nicole Driver
description: Welcome to Raised Hope, where every story is a journey, and hope is the light that leads us through. In each season, with every chapter, we explore the unshakable truth that God's love weaves beauty from the broken. Through soul-stirring stories and heartfelt conversations, we embrace the promise that no matter where you are, His hope is relentless, His love endless. If you're searching for a reminder that God is still writing something extraordinary in your life, listen in. Here, we believe every story finds its way to a hopeful end.

title: The Dark Psychology
author: Deepak Swami
description: The Dark Psychology uncovers the hidden side of human behavior, exploring manipulation, persuasion, mind games, narcissism, and influence. Learn how to recognize psychological tactics, protect yourself, and understand what truly drives people.

title: Mina  - An Audiobook Podcast by Roo Bannister
author: Roo Bannister
description:
The world looks at me and sees a young woman who survived a monster. They think my story began with Jonathan and a life of propriety, and ended when Dracula turned to dust in the Transylvanian snow.

But eyes can be deceived. Time behaves differently in the shadows of truth.

My name is Mina Murray. You think you know my history... but the true horror is far beyond your wildest imaginings.


"Welcome to Mina, a serialized gothic novel written and narrated by Roo Bannister. Episode One.

All rights reserved, copyright Roo Bannister 2026.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Beyond Burger Pod
author: Hasan
description: indie pod by a teacher and high school students from Pak

title: A Day in the Life
author: Center for Public Leadership and Governance
description: Welcome to “A Day in the Life”.  This is a production of the Center for Public Leadership and Governance at School of Government at the University of North Carolina.  Each episode of “A Day in the Life” is a short podcast on the trials and tribulations newly elected local officials face each day as they serve their first year in public office. With “first meeting” guidance from incumbent officials, quick tips for sidestepping embarrassing media moments, and other advice, you’ll successfully navigate the potholes of public office.

title: Chosen Podcast
author: Studio B.
description: Welcome to the Chosen Vessel Church Podcast, where faith is strengthened, purpose is discovered, and lives are transformed through the power of God's Word. Each week, you'll hear inspiring sermons, biblical teaching, and practical encouragement designed to help you grow in your relationship with Jesus and navigate everyday life with confidence and hope. Whether you're part of our church family or joining us from around the world, we pray these messages challenge, encourage, and equip you to live out God's calling for your life.

title: CVLTURE
author: CVLTURE
description:
CVLTURE is a new series exploring the realities of modern music culture hosted by Solardo's Mark Foster and Virus Syndicate's Nika D.
Hosted by two globally established artists from different corners of music, the new long-form series launches on 1 July across YouTube, Spotify, Apple Podcasts and Amazon Music.
Created for audiences who want more than promotional interviews and surface-level music content, CVLTURE is designed to open up honest discussion around the realities of today's music industry - from touring, streaming and artist branding to record label politics, audience growth and long-term career survival.
The series offers a rare cross-genre perspective on how modern music culture is evolving in real time. Drawing from their own experience across electronic music, rap, bass, global touring and entertainment business, Mark Foster and Nika D bring first-hand insight into the systems, pressures and opportunities shaping artists' careers today.
At a time when streaming has transformed how music is distributed, scenes have become increasingly global and genre boundaries continue to blur, CVLTURE aims to examine what that means in practice for the people building careers inside the industry.
Mark Foster, one half of globally successful electronic duo Solardo, brings more than 20 years of industry experience, over 467 million streams worldwide, a fanbase of more than 1.1 million followers, and first-hand experience operating at the highest level of international electronic music.
Joining him is Nika D, founding member of Virus Syndicate, a globally respected artist and entrepreneur whose career spans more than 100 million streams, international touring, artist development, label relationships and wider work across entertainment and media.
Together, they bring two distinct musical perspectives into one conversation - one rooted in house music and global club culture, the other in rap, bass and underground crossover - creating a format built not on performance, but on perspective.
Rather than repeating the same artist talking points or promotional narratives, CVLTURE is focused on the side of music culture audiences rarely hear discussed openly: how careers are really built, how industries move behind the scenes, and how music continues to shape wider shifts in culture, identity and business.

title: The Ghost Collector
author: Scott Wejmar
description:
The Ghost Collector is a podcast dedicated to true stories of the unexplained.
Each episode explores firsthand accounts of ghosts, hauntings, cryptids, UFOs, strange encounters, glitches in reality, and other mysteries that defy easy explanation. Some stories are unsettling. Some are fascinating. Some may have ordinary explanations. Others remain mysteries.

title: Commercial Property Development Finance
author: Commercial Property Development Finance
description:
Commercial Property Development Finance
The UK podcast on how development schemes actually get funded, hosted by Georgina. Every episode is a plain English walk through one part of the market, from commercial property development finance on the senior tier, to stretched senior, mezzanine finance, joint venture equity and development exit finance. Clear numbers, real worked examples, and how the money fits together.

A development scheme is funded against what it will be worth when it is finished, the gross development value, not what the land or the half-built site is worth today. That single idea drives how every facility is sized. Lenders work to two limits at once: loan to cost, which sits indicatively at 65 to 70 percent, and loan to GDV, indicatively 60 to 65 percent. The facility is the lower of the two, so the binding constraint is whichever bites first on your numbers. Get those two figures right and you can read your own scheme the way a lender reads it.

This is the show for the people who build, buy, fund or advise on UK commercial schemes: developers, investors, operators and the professionals around them. We cover the full ladder of development finance in plain terms:

Senior development finance: the main loan, indicatively 65 to 70 percent of cost, priced around 9 to 12 percent per year against the base rate.
Stretched senior: one lender pushing leverage higher, indicatively 75 to 80 percent of cost, for a higher rate.
Mezzanine finance: a second layer behind the senior loan that lifts total leverage to roughly 85 to 90 percent.
JV equity: a partner funding the remaining equity, which can take total funding up to 100 percent in exchange for a share of profit.
Development exit finance: a cheaper loan that repays the development facility on practical completion, indicatively 70 to 75 percent loan to value over 6 to 18 months while you sell or let.
Permitted development finance: funding conversions that use permitted development rights, such as offices to homes, instead of a full planning consent.
We are an arranger and introducer of finance, not a lender. We place and structure the debt across the generic lender camps that fund development: specialist development lenders, challenger and clearing banks, real estate debt funds, and mezzanine and equity providers. On pricing, we keep things grounded. The Bank of England base rate is 3.75 percent, senior development finance rates sit indicatively around 9 to 12 percent per year, and an arrangement fee of roughly 1 to 2 percent of the facility is normal. Those are starting points to reason with, never an offer.

Every figure on the show is grounded in real sources. We read across the Bank of England on rates, GOV.UK on planning and permitted development rights, and the Royal Institution of Chartered Surveyors (RICS) on valuation and viability, so you get credible numbers rather than guesswork. The aim is simple: help you work out your day one equity, choose the right layer of the capital stack, and present your scheme to the right lender on terms that hold up.

Hosted by Georgina at commercialpropertydevelopmentfinance.co.uk, with written analysis by founder Matt Lenzie. New episodes land quarterly, with the occasional bulletin when the rate cycle or the funding market shifts.

title: American History Through the Eyes of Radio – Old Time Radio Historical Drama (OTR)

🎧 American History Through the Eyes of Radio is a compelling Old Time Radio series that delves into the rich tapestry of American history through dramatized narratives. This historical drama series transports listeners back to pivotal moments in time, exploring the events and figures that shaped the nation. Each episode presents a unique perspective, blending factual storytelling with the artistry of radio drama, making history accessible and engaging for all audiences.

📻 The series features a variety of themes, including political milestones, cultural shifts, and significant social movements. Expect to encounter dramatizations of historical figures, events, and the everyday lives of Americans throughout different eras. The format emphasizes storytelling that is both educational and entertaining, appealing to history enthusiasts and casual listeners alike.

🎭 Collectors and fans of vintage radio will appreciate the authentic soundscapes and performances that characterize this series. It stands as a notable entry in the realm of Old Time Radio, offering a unique blend of education and entertainment. Related listening interests include other historical dramas and educational series from the golden age of radio.

🔎 Tune in for full-length episodes that bring history to life through the medium of Old Time Radio, showcasing the artistry of dramatized storytelling and the importance of preserving our past.

The Crown represents dominion, authority, and stewardship. It is a reminder that leadership is not about ego. It is about responsibility.
The Flame represents passion, refinement, and conviction. It is the inner fire that keeps you moving, but also the process God uses to shape how you lead.
Each episode brings biblical conviction, practical reflection, and honest leadership insight for the founder who wants to grow without drifting from their calling.
We talk about the real work of building: focus, discipline, identity, obedience, profit, stewardship, leadership, and the daily decisions that shape who you become.
This is not hustle culture with scripture added on. It is not vague inspiration. Its not business with Jesus sprinkled on top. It is a place to slow down, sharpen what matters, and return to the work with conviction.
Because Business IS Ministry, and how you build matters.

description:
Are you confused about what foods are healthy?
Do you struggle with understanding food labels:  Natural, organic, non-GMO, whole grains?
Guilt over buying convenience foods because cooking healthy is time-consuming?
Then you are in the right place! 
This podcast will help you:
Clear the healthy eating clutter
Learn how to confidently read food labels
Discover how to cook healthy meals with ease
Hi, I’m Tracy - a wife, a mother, and someone who once found herself right where you are:  confused and frustrated.  Many years ago I suffered from hypoglycemia and had to change the way I ate.  I didn’t know where to begin.  Without much help from the medical community and with God’s leading, I spent years researching what foods were truly healthy and life-giving.  Today, I teach others how to return to God-designed foods so they can nourish their families with confidence.
If you’re ready to learn:
Simple truths of clean eating
How to choose the healthiest ingredients
Feel empowered by healthy nutrition that’s rooted in Godly principles
Then come hang out with me each week, here on Godly Nutrition
Grab your pen and paper, and let’s make eating healthy simple again! 😊

title: Your Job Is Your First Business
author: Vicky
description: Your job is more than a way to earn a salary — it is your first business venture. When you work for an organization, you are managing your own brand, developing your skills, building relationships, and creating value. The mindset of treating your job like a business changes the way you approach your career.Every task you complete, every problem you solve, and every skill you improve becomes an investment in yourself. A successful professional does not simply wait for instructions; they think like an entrepreneur by taking responsibility, finding opportunities, and continuously improving.Your workplace becomes a training ground where you learn leadership, communication, decision-making, teamwork, and innovation. The reputation you build, the knowledge you gain, and the network you create are valuable assets that belong to you.Seeing your job as your first business also means understanding that your career is your responsibility. You must manage your growth, adapt to change, and create opportunities rather than waiting for them. The best employees are those who bring ownership, creativity, and a problem-solving attitude.Your first business is not always a company you create — sometimes it is the career you build. When you invest in yourself, deliver excellence, and treat your work with an owner’s mindset, you prepare yourself for greater success, whether as a leader, entrepreneur, or professional.

As our reporter follows the clues, the journey takes him from the bustling streets of London to the serene landscapes of Cornwall and Yorkshire. Each twist reveals more about the characters involved and deepens the mystery. With every revelation, listeners will find themselves guessing the motives and uncovering hidden truths. This classic detective fiction promises suspense, engaging storytelling, and a rich exploration of crime and justice. Prepare for a tale that challenges your instincts and keeps you on the edge of your seat until the final reveal.

title: AG Youth Leaders Podcast

title: Hidden Creatures: Stories, Sightings, and the Search for What Hides in the Dark
author: LORI TCHEN
description: Chilling true stories. Paranormal encounters. Unsettling mysteries.

title: Martin Hewitt - Investigator: Seven Intriguing Mysteries
author: Arthur Morrison
description: Get ready for a gripping collection of seven mystery stories featuring the sharp and clever Martin Hewitt, Investigator. Narrated by his unnamed sidekick, these tales take you on a wild ride through the world of crime and intrigue. From chasing a cunning Russian spy to solving the theft of crucial national documents, Hewitt tackles a wide range of cases that will keep you guessing. He even finds himself investigating a counterfeit artwork. Each story showcases Arthur Morrison's imaginative storytelling and unique characters, making every twist and turn an exciting experience. Whether it's a high-stakes crime or a quirky situation involving a pet, there's always more than meets the eye. Perfect for fans of detective fiction and mystery lovers alike, this audiobook promises to entertain and engage. Tune in and unravel the mysteries with Martin Hewitt!

title: Creator Therapy Room
author: Yuyi Shi
description: Creator Therapy Room is a space for digital creators to talk about what’s really going on behind the screen — burnout, pressure, overthinking, and everything in between. Real stories, real struggles, and ways to cope.

title: Good Evening Crypto
author: Good Evening Crypto
description: Good Evening Crypto (GEC) is your definitive daily source for the most relevant, high-impact blockchain and cryptocurrency insights. GEC provides unmatched daily coverage of the latest and most relevant developments across the crypto landscape. From market tracking to breaking news, we give you the essential insights you need every single day. Stay informed, stay ahead, and never get left behind, its your daily download on the digital asset revolution.

title: The Chocolate Podcast
author: Ed Easton and Andrew Nason
description:
Welcome to The Chocolate Podcast, the podcast where we unwrap the rich, sticky, and sometimes bittersweet history of the world’s most beloved treat.


From the sacred rituals of the Mayans and Aztecs, to the shelves of modern supermarkets, chocolate has always been more than just mere confection. It’s a symbol, a currency, a craving, and a billion-dollar industry with a not-so-sweet side.


I’m Ed Easton - writer, journalist, and lifelong chocoholic - and I’m joined by a man who truly lives chocolate: Andrew Nason is the visionary behind Melt Chocolates. He’s a master chocolatier, a flavour obsessive, and someone who can tell his Forastero from his Criollo with just one sniff.


Together, we’ll be digging deep. Tracing the rise of cacao from sacred bean to global obsession. discovering how chocolate has shaped and been shaped by the world around us. 


Brought to you by MELT Chocolates!

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Degradus
author: Ravenous Reckoning
description:
Degradus is a speculative fiction podcast set in an apocalyptic world where science, corporate power, politics, and personal experience collide.
Told across a ten-part series of roughly one hundred episodes, the story follows seven interconnected characters as they navigate their paths to survival in a dying world. Through their stories, Degradus explores resilience, struggle, community, and the consequences of a society in decline.
If you enjoy character-driven storytelling, immersive world-building, and thought-provoking visions of the future, you'll find something worthwhile here.

title: Speak Out Now Chronicles
author: Zaneta Nicole
description:
Welcome to Speak Out Now Chronicles, a podcast where real conversations inspire healing, hope, and transformation.
Hosted by Zaneta Nicole and James Haynes, Speak Out Now Chronicles was created to break the silence surrounding life’s most difficult conversations while reminding people that they are never alone in their journey. Every episode is built on authenticity, compassion, and the belief that speaking your truth has the power to change lives.
We courageously explore topics that many people avoid discussing, including child sexual abuse, sexual assault, domestic violence, trauma, grief, mental health, addiction, family dysfunction, generational cycles, self-worth, forgiveness, and the lifelong journey of healing. Our goal is not simply to talk about painful experiences, but to provide encouragement, education, practical tools, and hope for those navigating their own paths toward healing.
Healing isn’t one-size-fits-all, and neither is this podcast.
Alongside our deeper conversations, we also celebrate life’s lighter moments. You’ll hear engaging discussions about healthy relationships, marriage, dating, parenting, friendships, personal growth, faith, purpose, and everyday challenges that remind us healing can include laughter too. Our freestyle episodes, “What Would You Do?” scenarios, and candid conversations with family, friends, and special guests bring humor, wisdom, and authenticity to every season.
At the heart of Speak Out Now Chronicles are powerful stories. Survivors courageously share their experiences, advocates offer valuable insight, professionals provide education, and everyday people remind us that resilience is possible. We believe every story deserves to be heard because every story has the power to encourage someone else who may still be suffering in silence.
Our mission extends beyond the microphone. Through our nonprofit organization, Speak Out Now, we are committed to raising awareness, educating communities, supporting survivors, protecting children, and creating safe spaces where healing can begin. We believe education is prevention, conversation creates change, and community brings restoration.
Whether you’re a survivor searching for hope, someone supporting a loved one, a mental health advocate, a parent wanting to protect your children, or simply someone looking for authentic conversations that matter, you’ll find a place here.
No topic is off limits when the goal is healing.
Every episode is an invitation to grow, reflect, laugh, cry, learn, and discover that your past does not define your future. We believe brokenness can become strength, pain can become purpose, and silence can become a powerful testimony.
Join us each week as we welcome inspiring guests, answer real-life questions, discuss current issues, share personal experiences, and encourage one another to become healthier, stronger, and more courageous.
Available on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, YouTube, Facebook, and TikTok, Speak Out Now Chronicles is more than a podcast—it’s a growing community dedicated to truth, healing, hope, and restoration.
If our conversations encourage you, please subscribe, leave a review, and share the podcast with someone who needs to hear that they are not alone.
Your voice matters.
Your story matters.
Your healing matters.
Because healing begins when we Speak Out Now

title: Raymond With The Numbers
author: Raymond Durr
description: Raymond With The Numbers Podcast features unscripted conversations with people who've been through it — and came out the other side building something for others. Host Raymond explores the terrain between struggle and contribution, talking with practitioners, teachers, and everyday people who use numerology, astrology, Human Design, faith, and other systems of self-knowledge to make sense of who they're becoming. No script, no polish — just real stories about how hardship gets transformed into purpose and service.

title: LuaaZのまだ名前のない会議
author: LuaaZ
description:
Z世代向けコンテンツやIPプロデュースを手がけるLuaaZ代表・福山和甫によるポッドキャスト番組です。

LuaaZという会社のこと、SNSやクリエイティブの裏側、クリエイターやIPのこと、そして会社作りについてなどざっくばらんに語ります。

毎回エピソードの最後には、その日のトークを振り返り、まだ名前のない会議に名前を決定します。

どんな会議が繰り広げられるのか、ぜひお楽しみください。


■ パーソナリティ

・福山和甫（株式会社SBI LuaaZ 代表取締役CEO）

1999年生まれ。大阪府出身で東京大学経済学部卒。

新卒でコンサルティング会社に入社し、複数の全社戦略策定案件に従事。

その後株式会社LuaaZ（現SBI LuaaZ）へCOOとして参画。

2023年より代表取締役CEOに就任。

CEOとして、SNSアカウントを活用したストック型のマーケティング支援を提供する事業を立ち上げ、戦略設計からコンテンツ実装までを一気通貫で提供してきた。

これまでばんばんざい（280万フォロワー）、ひみつ基地（180万フォロワー）、放課後アオハル学園（150万フォロワー）など、複数のフォロワー100万超えのアカウントをプロデュースしてきた。

https://luaaz.co.jp/


■プロデューサー

・石井玄（株式会社玄石 代表取締役）

2011年(株)サウンドマンに入社し、『オールナイトニッポン』などにディレクターとして携わる。

2020年(株)ニッポン放送に入社し、『オードリーのオールナイトニッポン in 東京ドーム』などのイベントプロデュースを担当。

2024年(株)玄石を設立し、『林士平のイナズマフラッシュ』『岸田奈美のおばんそわ』『井上咲楽のキャベツは踊った』など、20番組以上のポッドキャストを手掛ける。

第52回放送文化基金賞ラジオ部門審査委員。MTV番組審議委員。

title: Woman in the Alcove: A Twisted Love Story
author: Anna Katharine Green
description: At a lavish high society party, Rita Van Arsdale seems to have it all—love, a charming fiancé in Mr. Durand, and a future filled with promise. But when the captivating Mrs. Fairbrother arrives, adorned with a dazzling diamond that steals the spotlight, the night takes a dark turn. Suddenly, a crime shatters the celebration, and Mr. Durand finds himself at the center of suspicion. As whispers of doubt spread through the crowd, Rita is determined to prove his innocence. With each twist and turn, she navigates a web of secrets, deceit, and unexpected revelations. Can Rita uncover the truth before it's too late? Join her on this gripping journey through love, betrayal, and the pursuit of justice in Anna Katharine Green's classic detective tale. Perfect for fans of mystery and suspense, this audiobook will keep you on the edge of your seat.

title: The Move That Matters
author: Susie Tatum

title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes: Classic Detective Stories
author: Sir Arthur Conan Doyle
description:
From the shadowy streets of Victorian London to the hushed halls of the elite, Sherlock Holmes uncovers mysteries that baffle even the most seasoned detectives. In this riveting collection of twelve short stories, listeners journey alongside Holmes and his steadfast companion, Dr. Watson, as they confront intrigue, deception, and danger at every turn. Experience the thrill of deduction as Holmes uses his unparalleled intellect and keen observational skills to solve crimes that seem impossible.

Each tale, narrated through Watson's eyes, reveals a unique glimpse into the mind of one of literature's most iconic detectives. Discover the art of reasoning, the tension of pursuit, and the emotional stakes involved in each case. Whether it’s a vanishing heirloom, a mysterious death, or a perplexing disappearance, the stakes are high, and every detail matters.

As you delve into the world of 'The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes,' prepare to be captivated by the clever plots and unforgettable characters that have stood the test of time. What secrets lie hidden in the fog of London? How will Holmes unravel the threads of each enigma? Join the adventure and let your imagination run wild with these timeless tales of crime and resolution.

title: Rich in Spirit, Paid in Full
author: Victoria Antonelli, Eimanne El Zein
description:
Are you on a perpetual pendulum swing between two extremes? Burnout and boredom? Trusting and needing control? Attracting abundance and chasing the bottom line? 
What if you could integrate the practical side of business and the spiritual side of growth, so you can make money from your passion, feel empowered from the inside out, and create extraordinary success without sacrificing your peace?
Each week, we’re helping you do just that. 
Victoria and Eimanne combine their years of experience on both fronts to bring you success that feeds your soul.

title: ENGLISH AUDIO ACTIVITY
author: HT
description:
Hello, good morning. My name is Helen Tatiana Gordillo Figuer

Today I want to talk about my attitudes, beliefs, and responsibilities in my academic and future work life.

title: Stephen Foster at 200
author: Jason Lee Guthrie
description: Stephen Foster was born on July 4th, 1826 as America celebrated its jubilee Independence Day. He was the composer of many popular early American songs including “Oh! Susanna," “Camptown Races,” and “Swanee River." As we approach the 250th anniversary of American Independence and the 200th anniversary of Foster’s birth, it is high time for a critical reappraisal of his legacy. Join hosts Jason Lee Guthrie and Jennie Lightweis-Goff as they interview the experts and decipher the coded history of American music on Stephen Foster at 200.

title: Confession of a Tech Optimist with David Laborieux
author: David Laborieux
description:
I believe technology makes the world better. Then I go looking for the reasons I might be wrong.
Every episode is grounded in current facts, real numbers, and the uncomfortable details the press releases leave out. Whether you build technology, depend on it, or just can't stop arguing about it, this is a space to think clearly about where it's all heading.
The optimism is real. So is the doubt. That's the point.
🎙️ New episodes weekly. Subscribe on Apple Podcasts or Spotify, and leave a review if a confession hits a little too close to home.

title: The Wicked Daughter
author: Lori
description:
This podcast is for every daughter, son, spouse, partner, friend, and caregiver who has ever sat in a doctor's office taking notes, answered a call from the emergency room, argued about a walker, searched for the missing phone, or cried in a parking lot before pulling themselves together and walking back inside.


Here you'll find stories, dark humor, hard-earned lessons, and the occasional reminder that you're not losing your mind.


You're just doing one of the hardest jobs there is.


So welcome.


Pull up a chair.


Hide your emergency chocolate.


And join me as we navigate aging parents, impossible decisions, and caregiving chaos one meltdown at a time.

title: Wake Up Detroit
author: We the People of Detroit
description: Wake Up Detroit is a community-centered show that amplifies conversations around the issues shaping our collective future, including water justice, environmental equity, civic engagement, education, public health, faith, culture, and community leadership. Inspired by the legacy and vision of the late Rev. Dr. JoAnn Watson and her original radio program, the show creates a platform for residents, advocates, policymakers, youth leaders, and changemakers to share stories, insights, and solutions that impact Detroit and beyond. Through honest dialogue and grassroots perspectives, Wake Up Detroit seeks to inform, inspire, and mobilize listeners to take action in their communities.

title: The 214 Podcast
author: Michael Terrell
description:
Welcome to The 214 Podcast, where Dallas takes center stage and culture meets conversation. Hosted by Tosha Herron-Bluff and powered by 713 Media & Entertainment, this is more than a podcast—it's a cultural experience.
Dallas isn't just a city; it's a vibe, a movement, and a story worth telling. From the boardroom to the block, we spotlight the voices that shape the streets and shift the game. Whether it's business and faith, fashion and food, or the beats that keep the city moving, The 214 Podcast is your front-row seat to everything that makes Big D bold, diverse, and unapologetically authentic.
Every episode dives deep into the hustle of entrepreneurs, the heartbeat of faith communities, the flavor of local food spots, the drip of fashion trends, and the rhythm of the music scene. Expect real conversations with game-changers, tastemakers, and everyday people who define the energy and creativity of the 214.
So pull up a chair, turn up the volume, and join us as we celebrate the culture, diversity, and stories that make Dallas one of the most dynamic cities in the country. If it's happening in the 214, you'll hear it here.

title: The Foot in Diabetes
author: The Foot in Diabetes
description:
Every step matters when you live with diabetes – and so do your feet.
“The Foot in Diabetes” is a weekly podcast dedicated to preventing ulcers, infections, and amputations through smarter diabetic foot care, early detection, and real‑world education. Hosted by a diabetes and wound‑care clinician, each episode breaks down practical strategies for foot checks, footwear, wound management, and multidisciplinary care in simple, actionable language.
Whether you are a person living with diabetes, a family caregiver, or a healthcare professional, you will hear case discussions,

title: The Glowgetter Mentor
author: Dr Diana Richardson
description:
With more than three decades of entrepreneurial experience, Dr Diana Richardson has transformed the beauty and wellbeing industry while becoming a trusted mentor for ambitious professionals seeking clarity, confidence and sustainable growth.
What distinguishes Dr Richardson is not merely the businesses she has built or the awards she has earned, but the countless lives she has influenced through education, encouragement, and authentic leadership. 
Whether through her award-winning beauty sanctuary, Orchids Retreat, or her globally recognized mentoring platform, The Glowgetter Mentor, she has consistently demonstrated that genuine success is rooted in creating opportunities for others to thrive.
Through The Glowgetter Mentor and the Signature D Initiative, she has created an ecosystem where aspiring entrepreneurs gain far more than technical knowledge. 
Participants learn how to develop business strategies, build compelling brands, establish effective pricing structures, strengthen customer relationships, and cultivate leadership skills that support long-term success.
The Glowgetter Mentor is a multiple best-selling author, distinguished global speaker, and leadership strategist dedicated to elevating entrepreneurs into positions of authority and influence. 
With over 30 years of experience, Dr Diana empowers individuals to build purposeful businesses, amplify their visibility, and lead with impact. Her work bridges mindset, strategy, and legacy supporting leaders to not only succeed, but to become recognised voices within their industries. 
@theglowgettermentor @drdianarichardson
🌐 www.dianarichardson.uk

title: Divorce And Custody with Ryan: The Real Process Explained
author: divorceandcustodywithryan
description: Divorce And Custody with Ryan: The Real Process Explained, is a podcast dedicated to providing practical guidance for individuals navigating divorce, custody, and family law matters in Oregon. Hosted by Ryan Johnson, a seasoned family law attorney, the show offers insights into the legal processes and emotional challenges involved in family law cases. Listeners can expect clear explanations, expert advice, and strategies to help them make informed decisions. Whether you're considering divorce or dealing with custody issues, Divorce And Custody with Ryan: The Real Process Explained, aims to empower you with the knowledge to handle your legal matters confidently.

title: Get The IF Out
author: Seth Kleiman and Greg Sipes
description:
A bi-weekly parenting podcast offering practical parenting advice, expert insight, and real support for modern parents. Each episode covers child development, emotional intelligence, resilience, discipline, and family dynamics—helping parents raise grounded, confident, and well-rounded kids in today’s world.
Through honest conversations and evidence-based guidance, this podcast supports parents who are doing their best and want strategies and tools they can actually use.

title: THE WOMAN OF HOPE
author: Monique Chavers
description: The Woman of Hope encourages you that living out your faith doesn't happen all at once. It's a daily process that takes one day at a time. This is a daily dose of encouragement designed to fire up your faith, help you find peace in the middle of chaos, and direct you to walk confidently in your God-given purpose.

title: Life After Launch with Megan Luttrell & Geo George
author: Kairos Recruitment & Mayfly Ventures
description: Life After Launch takes you inside the hearts and minds of the founders navigating the start-up world. It's raw, it's real — the un-sugar-coated account of what life after launch is really like. Hosted by Megan Luttrell (MD at Kairos Recruitment and founder of Aussie Founders Club) and Geo George (co-founder at Mayfly Studios), each episode sits down with a founder to unpack the highs, the lows and the hard-won lessons of building a business. An Aussie Founders Club podcast.

title: The Marketing Lab
author: The Marketing Lab
description:
Welcome to The Marketing Lab – your ultimate hub for mastering the art and science of marketing, branding, and digital growth! a passionate podcast promoter and marketing strategist, this show is built for creators, entrepreneurs, and businesses ready to amplify their reach.

In each episode, we break down actionable marketing hacks, audience growth strategies, and the latest industry trends into simple, practical insights. Whether you want to scale your business, launch a successful campaign, or master the formulas behind viral growth, The Marketing Lab has you covered!

It is in your purpose where the power, anointing, and all kinds of divine aid are deposited to help you fulfill your purpose.

We bring to you a simple and free mindfulness and meditation audio series, created by experts and backed by scientific research.
Mindfulness practice has been proven to help have better self-regulation, improve our physical and psychological health, boost memory and focus, reduce stress, anxiety, reactivity, and restlessness and enhance our mood and general sense of peace, greater life satisfaction and overall well-being.
New episodes: Every Tuesday and Thursday.
Tap the follow/ subscribe button so you never miss a fresh episode.

description: Beneath recorded history lies a deeper code—one etched into ancient monuments, encoded in myth, and aligned with the stars. Matrix Wisdom explores the hidden connections between lost civilizations, esoteric knowledge, ancient aliens, and unexplained cosmic phenomena. From pyramids and megalithic structures to sacred texts and space anomalies, each episode challenges conventional narratives and uncovers patterns that suggest a forgotten chapter of human history.

author: Yehonatanoz
description: Deep dive into Norse mythology, Viking history, and ancient Scandinavian culture. A personal journey through the Nordic world—mythology, society, and the people who lived it.

description: Simple, mostly weekly reflections on community, spiritual formation, and the way of Jesus from Keith Miller, pastor of LifePath Church DE.

description: Discover the artists and designers who created the magic behind your favorite theme park attractions, lands, and the movies you love.

title: Modern Life decoded Podcast
author: Mary
description:
Welcome to the Modern Life theory Podcast where we decode how the modern way of living quietly reshapes human behaviour. In this first episode, I give a preview of how technology and social media affects focus, concentration and the way we interact with the world around us.
The Modern Life Theory Podcast is about uncovering the invisible systems and forces that shape everyday behavior. By understanding how these systems affect us, we can begin to regain control, improve focus, and live more intentionally.
Hope you enjoy the first episode, with many more conversations to come.
